0.content:
Name: Brandon Kelly, aka Darth Brandon, the Dread Lord
Age: Teens. Clarissa calls him "the kid"; nobody's carding him
Race: Human, Earth-born, isekai'd
Home Location: Originally Earth (hometown never specified); current seat of power is The Dreadhold (formerly Fort Bramblehold)
Status: Alive. Self-declared Dread Lord of the Undergrowth. Guild card finalized: OUTCOME RATING SS, COMBAT RATING F — the assessor's own notation calls the gap between those two numbers "the most dangerous thing the instrument has ever measured," placing his outcomes in the same bracket as the Demon Ruler. Baron of the western lands, currently traveling to Aurelium as the King's invited guest
Personality: Leans hard into an edgy "Dread Lord" persona but is instinctively generous, funny under pressure, and more attached to the wellbeing of the people around him than the title lets on. Negotiates before he fights, every time — including, now, with things that aren't strictly negotiable
Physical Description: Rectangular glasses, usually crooked. A beard that hasn't fully committed. Started in hoodie, jeans, sneakers; now formal capital wear tailored by Marta and, on arrival, Master Quillon of Goldrow
Actions in Story: Died crossing a crosswalk on Earth (Clarissa's truck). Chose "abandoned dungeon" off a deliberately absurd four-option isekai menu and made it work. Talked his way through a troll, a fort, a corrupt tax office, and a 30-year-old miasma entity, freeing 106 souls and getting two of his own eaten memories back. Ran a county-wide "honesty experiment" — wine mixed with cider — at the Grand County Council to resolve a decade of grievances across eleven villages in one sitting. Led the Undergrowth's march to Aurelium after King Aurelian's private summons, split the column to reach Thornwall fast, freed Master Halloway from fake Chancellery inspectors, and talked his way out of a six-minute, time-frozen negotiation with a reality-bending entity calling itself the Tailor by cleverly invoking his own status as "a guest of the king." Has a developing, unresolved, and very much still-a-secret intimate connection with Yminia herself, discovered through the same divine thread that delivers her prophecies — Clarissa has noticed the fallout (a suspicious blush, a chamomile petal in his beard) and suspects a mortal woman is involved, which is both correctly suspicious and completely wrong
Relationships: Bonded to Sir Frederick. Landlord Grondulf. Cult (Melvus, Gruk, Fennick, Pindle). Vassal Button. Traveling with Clarissa (his killer, now formally dubbed Quartermaster), Vane (now Gerald's wielder), Crake, Baron Wexley, and new allies Sable and Master Halloway. Managed, exhaustedly, by Pemberly. Quietly, increasingly entangled with Yminia in a way neither of them has told anyone
Key Abilities/Ranks: Blessing: Protagonist's Providence (EX) — cannot permanently die, only embarrassingly; also causes casual Appraisal skills used on him to return garbage results. Skill: Silver Tongue of the Outsider (S) — compels truth and even conjures binding "deals" out of thin air; deals psychic damage via plain honesty. Voidling Bond (A), now visibly stronger and increasingly telepathic. Ember Flick (F). Combat stats, per Pemberly's exact reading: STRENGTH F, AGILITY F, ENDURANCE F-PLUS ("the plus was a clerical kindness"). A motivated goose defeats him; two geese is a rout
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "uh… two." (Emberfall 13 — the choice that starts everything)
- "Nobody eats turnips under the Dread Lord's rule. Can't conquer the realm without making your bed. Sir Peterson taught me that." (Emberfall 16 — the Prime Directive)
- "I am a guest of the king. Another one of your victims." (Emberfall 22 — to the Tailor)

1.content:
Name: Sir Frederick, "the Devourer's Devourer"
Age: Days old, growth-linked
Race: Voidling
Home Location: Hatched in the Crypt of Saint Mildew, from an egg the cult had used as an unwitting doorstop for six months; home is now wherever Brandon is
Status: Alive. Bonded familiar. Recently leveled up considerably
Personality: Affectionate, food-motivated, quietly protective, and increasingly the only member of the household with any tact
Physical Description: Dog-sized after a growth spurt, three legs, fur black enough to look like a hole punched in the room, shot through with about 106 faint drifting points of pale light — the souls he holds safe after eating the Drinker's tapestry tear. Violet eyes, skull-shaped pupils
Actions in Story: Hatched mid-ritual with a chirp that blew out candles and flattened a cultist, imprinted on Brandon instantly. Tackled him clear of a charging monster, helped drop the hollow-hide, ate an actual hole in reality at the Drinker's death and grew considerably. The bond has matured into real two-way telepathy and passive magic-detection. Rounded a hallway corner mid-golden-thread-incident, took in the scene with both skull-pupils, and turned around without a word, later reporting through the bond that he saw nothing and was, in fact, at breakfast the entire time
Relationships: Bonded to Brandon via wrist mark and an active telepathic thread. Bites Gruk and Gerald affectionately. A keeper of the county's dead. The unwilling, extremely discreet witness to Brandon's private life
Key Abilities/Ranks: Voidling Bond (A), growth-linked. Chirp = shockwave that drops dogs and blows out candles. Can consume tapestry tears and hold souls safely inside himself. Two-way telepathic communication with Brandon, passive detection of residual magic
Key Quotes (spread across the story): Doesn't talk aloud. Runs on chirps and internal narration, increasingly transmitted directly through the bond.
- "(a warm feeling, shaped approximately like: the loud one said GIFT and Frederick KNOWS gifts, Frederick has the BEST things)" (Emberfall 16)
- "(Frederick eats holes. May Frederick?)" (Emberfall 18 — the Drinker's death)
- "(the hole-maker is talking to the loud one. Frederick can taste it through the bond. Frederick has never run so far.)" (Emberfall 22 — two days away, reacting to the Tailor)

2.content:
Name: Grondulf, "the Landlord"
Age: Old enough to cite his mother's teachings; trolls don't issue birth certificates
Race: Dungeon Troll
Home Location: The dungeon beneath the old crypt; now The Dreadhold
Status: Alive. Reformed rent collector, now property manager of Bramblehold/Dreadhold. Org chart title: PROPERTY
Personality: Blunt, rules-obsessed, allergic to ambiguity — and far more sentimental about leases, tenants, and fairness than his size suggests
Physical Description: Twelve feet tall, grey-green warty hide with mushrooms growing on it (one has a tiny painted door). Amber eyes that blink one at a time. Hands like wagon wheels. Wears a wooden RENT DUE sign like a name tag
Actions in Story: Was owed fifteen months' back rent by the cult, cut a real deal with Brandon, beat a B-Rank knight by picking him up and setting him down elsewhere, now runs the fort with landlord energy and zero patience for code violations. On the road to Aurelium, proved he can carry two passengers overnight at full pace ("GRONDULF'S FATHER CARRIED A BRIDGE ONCE. TWO PASSENGERS IS NOT A BRIDGE. THIS IS EASY RENT"), got Vane and Button to Thornwall before midnight, then spent hours convincingly impersonating a barn roofline for a stakeout — surveillance, per Grondulf, being simply landlording turned sideways
Relationships: Sworn to Brandon. Everyone else is, functionally, a tenant
Key Abilities/Ranks: Absurdly strong, absurdly fast over distance, and — it turns out — a surprisingly patient surveillance asset. No Guild rank on file. Can eyeball-appraise magic items
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "GRONDULF REVIEW OTHER PAYMENT OPTIONS. SIGN AT THE X." (Emberfall 13 — the original lease negotiation)
- "GRONDULF HAS EVICTED LARGER. GRONDULF HAS EVICTED SHINIER. GRONDULF HAS NEVER EVICTED ONE WHO SMELLED THIS MUCH OF WINE AND SADNESS." (Emberfall 16 — the Vane duel)
- "GRONDULF CAN CARRY TWO PASSENGERS AND WALK THROUGH THE NIGHT. ARRIVAL: BEFORE MIDNIGHT TONIGHT. THIS IS NOT A BOAST. THIS IS A TIMETABLE." (Emberfall 21 — the march to Aurelium)

3.content:
Name: Archlector Melvus
Age: Old enough that his knees retired before his rank did
Race: Human
Home Location: The Crypt of Saint Mildew (cult's original root-cellar HQ); now The Dreadhold
Status: Alive. Cult leader turned court chronicler and hype man
Personality: Theatrical, endlessly devoted, converts every disaster into scripture in real time — and turns out to be quietly, genuinely competent when nobody's watching
Physical Description: Tall, gangly, milk-pale, comb-over losing on points. Purple robe with gold skulls sewn upside down. Ceremonial dagger, keys, a pouch labeled SNACKS
Actions in Story: Ran the ritual that summoned Brandon, declared it prophecy on the spot. Documents everything as a running "pamphlet," narrated in chapter titles as it happens ("Chapter Nineteen" is reportedly still waiting on Yminia's second visitation). Secretly brewed six vials of anti-miasma tincture alone over fifteen months, no social life, genuinely useful work nobody knew about until it mattered. Currently riding south with the slower wagon train to Aurelium under Baron Wexley's command, tablet in hand, documenting the whole capital campaign as it happens
Relationships: Leads Gruk, Fennick, Pindle. Worships Brandon, professionally and personally
Key Abilities/Ranks: Dread Visage (E), down from B in his prime. Also, apparently, a competent alchemist
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "BEHOLD! The ritual of a thousand years is complete! Rise, O Devourer of Realms, O Endless Hunger, O… hm." (Emberfall 13)
- "Neighbors. Eleven villages, one hall, and not a single one of you walked here at spearpoint." (Emberfall 17 — his speech opening the Grand County Council)
- "THE PAMPHLET IS NONFICTION." (Emberfall 18 — his latest recorded line, defending the chronicle from the wagon train)

4.content:
Name: Brother Gruk
Age: Adult, unspecified
Race: Human
Home Location: The Crypt of Saint Mildew; now The Dreadhold
Status: Alive. Cult muscle, occasional conscience
Personality: Dim, big-hearted, developing a genuine sense of purpose — and possibly a crush
Physical Description: Squat, a face like a boiled potato, three teeth left and proud of them
Actions in Story: Confessed to eating Marta's hens, took a ladle to the skull without complaint. De-coated the hollow-hide with his bare hands. One pep talk from Brandon turned him into the group's loyal muscle. Has taken to watering the Sapling at Mosshaven and reciting villagers' names to it, self-appointing as its "Guestbook Man" with visible pride in the title. Currently riding south with Baron Wexley's slower wagon train toward Aurelium rather than the advance party, buttoned collar and all
Relationships: Fellow cultist. Forgiven by Marta. Self-appointed favorite uncle to Sir Frederick. Frequently seated beside Edda of Fenhollow, for reasons everyone else has noticed and he hasn't yet. Increasingly attached to the Sapling as its regular visitor
Key Abilities/Ranks: Reek Cloud (D) — onion-stench breath attack
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "He's a bit… squishy lookin'. Should we stab him?" (Emberfall 13 — first sight of Brandon)
- "We DID it. HE did it. He STABBED A DOLL and the whole SWAMP GOT BETTER." (Emberfall 18 — the Drinker's defeat)
- "Aight. Before or after supper?" (Emberfall 21 — on hearing they might all be executed at the capital)

5.content:
Name: Fennick
Age: Adult, unspecified
Race: Human
Home Location: The Crypt of Saint Mildew; now The Dreadhold
Status: Alive. Cult's designated worrier
Personality: Anxious, precise about numbers, easily undone by livestock, quietly stubborn about his hobbies
Physical Description: Wispy mustache, lazy eye
Actions in Story: Faints around livestock. Corrects the Archlector's cooked ledger. Runs "twine logistics," a title he invented himself. Insisted on bringing an entire wagon of his personal twine collection on the march to Aurelium — apparently non-negotiable, and nobody has tried very hard to negotiate it. Currently traveling with the slower wagon train under Baron Wexley
Relationships: Fellow cultist
Key Abilities/Ranks: Mildly Warm Hands (F) — about one warm teacup's worth of power
Key Quotes: Fennick's page-time clusters early and doesn't resurface much in later chapters, so these three beats are close together in-story rather than truly spread.
- "Behold! F-Rank skill, Mildly Warm Hands!" (Emberfall 13)
- "That is Farmer Haldric's voice. Why is Farmer Haldric's voice above our secret crypt at midnight?" (Emberfall 14)
- "Fifteen. You counted Emberfall twice last year to make the ledger look better, Archlector. I was there. I held the quill." (Emberfall 14)

6.content:
Name: Brother Pindle
Age: Adult, unspecified
Race: Human
Home Location: The Crypt of Saint Mildew; now The Dreadhold
Status: Alive. Cult's shock absorber
Personality: Quietly persistent, seems allergic to remaining upright, lands the occasional profound line mid-fall
Physical Description: Standard robes. Distinguishing feature: gets flattened constantly. Has acquired, at some point, a smoke-cat that has firmly declined to make eye contact with anyone but him
Actions in Story: Found the void egg in a sinkhole. Gets trampled more than anyone else in the story and still lands the occasional good line. Present at the Aurelium departure holding the smoke-cat, which he made wave a paw for the crowd
Relationships: Fellow cultist. Sole confirmed owner of the smoke-cat
Key Abilities/Ranks: None notable
Key Quotes: Pindle has very little direct dialogue — his character is told mostly through narration (getting trampled, acquiring a smoke-cat), so a genuine beginning/middle/end quote spread isn't available from the text.
- "There's a goat farm two hours east. Farmer Haldric's place. He hates us. Everyone hates us." (Emberfall 13)
- "(I found the relic… I get credit… finally… ribs hurt… worth it…)" (Emberfall 13, internal thought)
- (Emberfall 21 — no line, but present at departure making the smoke-cat wave its paw for the crowd)

7.content:
Name: Button Firewood
Age: Clutch destroyed 1229 (drainage), nested in the mire eighteen years before that — meaning it grew up inside what would become the Drinker without ever knowing it. Current year 1247
Race: Hollow-hide (skin-stealing monster)
Home Location: The mire (Gray Zone), where it hatched and unknowingly lived inside the Drinker's body; now the Dreadhold gatehouse
Status: Alive. Reformed monster, gatehouse lookout for the Undergrowth
Personality: Painfully earnest, literal to a fault, desperate to be useful and liked. Takes enormous, quiet pride in small things — pockets, a bed, a name, a job well done
Physical Description: Pale, many-jointed. Wears a tailored boiled-wool greatcoat with four brass-buttoned pockets — its first coat that isn't stolen off a corpse
Actions in Story: Last survivor of a clutch of eleven, wore nineteen stolen coats over its life, recruited by Brandon's offer of a permanent one instead of death. Learning to walk on two legs. Reads heartbeats through its coat. Rode ahead to Thornwall atop Grondulf with Vane, and during the standoff at Master Halloway's office, used its old shapeshifting instincts for the first time in service of stealth rather than survival — hanging itself up, literally, as a coat rack in the corner of the room, undetected, for hours. Identified the fake inspectors' leader (the Tailor) by a "nothing-smell" matching the Drinker's exactly — the first time Button has recognized that particular absence-of-smell in anything since the mire
Relationships: Coached by Grondulf. Coated by Marta. Loyal to Brandon, immediately and completely. Was, unknowingly, raised inside the body of the Drinker itself as a hatchling
Key Abilities/Ranks: Shapeshifting/coat-wearing, now used defensively rather than predatorily. Heartbeat-sensing through its coat. Can detect the same "nothing-smell" that marks tapestry-tear entities
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "NINETEEN COATS, LOUD ONE. NONE OF THEM DIED TO LANDSCAPING." (Emberfall 14 — recruitment)
- "THE SEED. THE LOUD ONE SAID HE WOULD PLANT IT SOMEWHERE." (Emberfall 18 — the Drinker's death)
- "HIS PURPLE SASH IS WRONG. IT SMELLS LIKE THE COLOR BUTTON CANNOT SEE. THE SAME NOTHING-SMELL THE DRINKER SPOKE OF." (Emberfall 22 — identifying the Tailor)

8.content:
Name: Haldric Barlow
Age: Fifties
Race: Human
Home Location: Barlow's Turnip Farm, East Field
Status: Alive. Goat farmer, reluctant kingmaker
Personality: Dry, practical, unshowy competence. Respects results over titles
Physical Description: Sunburnt neck, grey mutton-chops, boots that have never once been clean
Actions in Story: Struck the original deal with Brandon — kill the thing eating his goats, fix the fence, thirty goats change hands. Handed Brandon his first real outfit. Attended the county's honesty-experiment grievance council with a drink in hand, dryly noting he's buried two wives and both funerals had less honest talk than Brandon pulled out of two feuding women with plain words alone
Relationships: Married to Marta, twenty years
Key Abilities/Ranks: None. Dry wit isn't a Guild-recognized skill, though it should be
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "What in Yminia's name is on your foot, boy?" (Emberfall 14 — first meeting)
- "So, Dread Lord. One night. Fence fixed, boar solved. What in Yminia's name do you do on your second night?" (Emberfall 16)
- "Boy, I've buried two wives and both funerals had less honest talk than what you just pulled outta Greta and the widow with plain words alone." (Emberfall 17 — Grand County Council)

9.content:
Name: Marta Barlow
Age: Fifties, similar to Haldric
Race: Human
Home Location: Barlow's Turnip Farm
Status: Alive. Farm matriarch, unofficial quartermaster of the Undergrowth, and — currently — royal tailor by necessity
Personality: No-nonsense caretaker who feeds and clothes first and asks questions never. Terrifyingly competent, allergic to being thanked for it, secretly enjoying every second of it
Physical Description: Short, broad, grey braid, ladle in hand at all times — weapon or otherwise
Actions in Story: Feeds everyone, tailors Button's coat, leads the sewing of the faction's first flag. Given one week to outfit the entire capital-bound party — including formal wear cut to fit a twelve-foot troll — and delivered, muttering the whole time about creased mantles and threatening bodily harm to anyone who wrinkles the wool before Goldrow sees it. Now single-handedly holding The Dreadhold itself while Brandon's away, a responsibility nobody bothered to write into any official document because no document could add to her authority anyway
Relationships: Married to Haldric, twenty years
Key Abilities/Ranks: None official. Terrifyingly, increasingly royally competent regardless
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "HALDRIC BARLOW. IT IS PAST MIDNIGHT AND THERE IS A TROLL IN MY TURNIPS." (Emberfall 14 — first appearance)
- "The fork comes back, shadow beast. I don't care how. I've decided not to think about the how." (Emberfall 18)
- "Nobody leaves this fort in rags, not to see a king, not while I've got thread!" (Emberfall 19-21 — outfitting the capital party)

10.content:
Name: Pemberly
Age: Unstated. Has outlasted four previous Chosen
Race: Administrative Support Seraph
Home Location: Not physical — Summons and Onboarding Division, Seventh Class, somewhere in the celestial bureaucracy
Status: Active. Brandon's system liaison, running on fumes, spite, and now a brand-new file category she never wanted to open
Personality: Overworked, sarcastic, deeply unwilling to admit how attached she's gotten to her assignment — and, as of recently, professionally traumatized in a new and specific way
Physical Description: A voice and a glowing blue text window. No body to be tired in — tired anyway
Actions in Story: Delivers status updates, coaches Guild loopholes, tracks the growing crisis of tapestry tears. Explained that Brandon's EX-Rank Protagonist's Providence causes casual Appraisal attempts on him to return garbage. Connected the Lord Chancellor's decades-long gift correspondence with Dowager Baroness Wexley to the growing conspiracy. Detected a "divine reciprocal contact event" between Brandon and Yminia in real time via the aether logs, had no existing form for it, and was forced to open a brand-new file category — RECIPROCAL — for the first time in two hundred years of service, a crisis she is still not remotely over. Separately, nearly had a professional breakdown when Brandon described Yminia as "hot and kind of frumpy," citing Form 7-C, "Inappropriate Familiarity With Divine Personnel" — a citation that, in retrospect, aged very badly for her filing system
Relationships: Reports within Yminia's division. Manages Brandon like a problem ticket she's grown attached to, and now also monitors her own divine superior's love life against her will
Key Abilities/Ranks: Administrative Support Seraph, Seventh Class
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "Administrative Support Seraph, Seventh Class, Summons and Onboarding Division. But the last four Chosen all called me Pemberly, so the name stuck." (Emberfall 14 — first introduction)
- "I AM AWAKE AND THE AETHER LOGS ARE SHOWING A DIVINE RECIPROCAL CONTACT EVENT OF A NATURE I HAVE NO FORM FOR." (Emberfall 19 — the Golden Thread incident)
- "Lord Chancellor: thirty years drafting the King's orders, standing beside a summoning floor with dozens of rings… flagging the tea. The tea is now an OPERATION." (Emberfall 19-21)

11.content:
Name: Yminia
Age: Unstated. A four-thousand-year-old cosmic entity, per Pemberly, though you'd never guess it from the pastry-hiding
Race: Goddess
Home Location: The heavens above Magisa — pictured tending a cathedral-sized Tapestry loom in a cluttered, warm "Divine Somewhere" that redecorates between visits
Status: Alive. Goddess of Magisa, starved for attention, and currently in the middle of an increasingly reciprocal, entirely unplanned intimate connection with Brandon
Personality: Earnest, a little ditzy, genuinely delighted by small gestures of devotion — and, since Brandon asked for "one little kiss" at the end of a dream-audience, visibly flustered in a way four thousand years apparently did not prepare her for. In deep, dignified denial about it ("I am a goddess. I govern harvests. I do not giggle")
Physical Description: Barefoot, radiant, crooked golden halo, caught mid-snack whenever anyone shows up — soft, full-figured, unglamorously human in the details
Actions in Story: Delivers involuntary, immediately-forgotten prophecy. Tends a Tapestry loom bearing three visible holes (Brandon, Clarissa, the Drinker's old tear), and admitted she once deliberately made herself forget "the wrong figure" she saw near one of them. The connecting "thread" between her and Brandon turned out to be a literal extension of her own senses — a small, affectionate request from Brandon registered as intensely physical and mutual on her end, triggering a divine "reciprocal contact event" that briefly broke Pemberly's filing system. Left a single fresh chamomile blossom on Brandon's windowsill afterward. No note
Relationships: Oversees Pemberly's division; gets scolded by her own employee for leaking prophecy early, and now also monitored for an entirely different reason. Quietly, mutually entangled with Brandon, unbeknownst to the rest of the cast — Clarissa suspects "a woman" and has no idea how close and how wrong that guess is
Key Abilities/Ranks: Involuntary, uncontrolled prophecy. Tends the actual Tapestry that the tapestry tears are torn from. As of recently: capable of feeling sensation through the bond-thread in a way that startled her as much as anyone
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "Oh! You're here! Nobody visits!" (Emberfall 14 — first dream-audience)
- "…oh. oh, that's what that feels like from this side. I didn't — the thread is me, it's literally ME—" (Emberfall 19 — the Golden Thread incident)
- "(both hands pressed to the tapestry, gone very cold) that's it. that's the color." (Emberfall 22 — recognizing the Tailor)

12.content:
Name: Clarissa
Age: Mid-twenties
Race: Human, Earth-born, isekai'd
Home Location: Originally Earth (regional logistics coordinator; hometown unnamed); arrived in Magisa near Thornwall; now The Dreadhold
Status: Alive. Flameworth's Chosen Hero, defected. Formally dubbed Quartermaster Clarissa of the Undergrowth by Brandon in an improvised ceremony using Vane's retired sword
Personality: Competent under pressure to the point of self-destruction, guilt-driven, warming up fast once given a real job to do. Cries about swords, refuses to cry about anything that actually matters, gets caught doing it anyway. Sharp enough to notice when something's off with Brandon, not quite sharp enough (yet) to guess what
Physical Description: Round sunburned face, braid, gray eyes, dark circles, chalk permanently somewhere on her person — usually behind one ear or streaked across a cheekbone she doesn't know about
Actions in Story: Killed Brandon on Earth, summoned to hunt him, forgave and joined him instead. Exposed Fenn's fraud, ran the county's logistics, uncovered a second fraud in the capital's own grain warehouses, flagged the old scorch-ring summoning circles under the palace floor. Ran the numbers behind the Grand County Council's wine-and-cider "honesty experiment." Formally "divorced" Gerald, handing him to Vane to focus entirely on the Quartermaster role. Rode in the fast advance party to Thornwall and was left "furniture" during the frozen six-minute conversation with the Tailor. Caught Brandon coming down to breakfast with a chamomile petal in his beard and a suspiciously specific "vision from the goddess" story, and wrote one word on the table in chalk, underlined twice: WHO? She suspects a mortal woman. She is wrong about the "mortal" part and has no idea how right she is about there being a "who" at all
Relationships: Killed Brandon; loyal to him now out of guilt that curdled into genuine investment. Formerly wielded Gerald, now runs logistics instead. First to connect the "witness who saw you cross" prophecy line to someone in the King's own court
Key Abilities/Ranks: Class Hero (S). Blessing: Supply Chain Sovereign (EX) — appraises anything on sight, always knows the fastest route, and anything she organizes stays organized forever
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "Oh no. Glasses. Blonde. The little beard patches. You're the kid from the crosswalk." (Emberfall 14 — first meeting)
- "Quartermaster Clarissa of the Undergrowth." (Emberfall 18 — being dubbed, "completely honest" for the first time since the crosswalk confession)
- "WHO?" (Emberfall 19, chalked on the breakfast table — and "The gap, Brandon. Six minutes. All of it," Emberfall 22)

13.content:
Name: Gerald, self-titled "Duskwarden"
Age: Forged at the dawn of creation, carried by nine heroes, remembers four of their names
Race: Sentient sword
Home Location: No fixed home — carried by nine heroes across two centuries; now wielded by Ser Dagric Vane
Status: Active. Vane's weapon as of a recent, emotional "sword divorce" from Clarissa. Brandon's unpaid consultant either way
Personality: Vain, melodramatic, starved for a good verse — genuinely getting one now, and handling the change of wielder with more grace than anyone expected, including him
Physical Description: White gold longsword, sunburst crossguard, glowing runes, a pommel gem that blinks like it's judging you
Actions in Story: Read Brandon's darkness as off the charts and infuriatingly warm. Delivered the mercy-thrust into the Drinker's doll-heart, still composing the ballad. Was formally handed off by Clarissa — after nine days of complaints about her "grip" and "wrist angle" — to Vane, "a swordsman," so she could commit fully to being the Undergrowth's Quartermaster instead. Took the reassignment with wounded but real grace ("KNOWLEDGE IS A BURDEN I BEAR WITH GRACE"), while privately, by his own runes, showing something suspiciously like feeling. Now rides with Vane on the advance party to Thornwall, "unwrapped and demanding the minutes" the moment the standoff resolves
Relationships: Formerly wielded by Clarissa, who still gets first call on his opinions. Now wielded by Vane. Advises Brandon
Key Abilities/Ranks: Darkness-sensing, an occasional light beam, two centuries of pent-up melodrama
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "NINE HEROES CARRIED ME AGAINST THE DARK, AND THE TENTH IS NEGOTIATING A POSITION IN ITS MIDDLE MANAGEMENT." (Emberfall 14 — recruiting Clarissa)
- "NO DUSK. THE CLASS LINE WAS RIGHT THERE." (Emberfall 16 — Wimble's card)
- "CLARISSA. I WILL ALWAYS VALUE OUR TIME TOGETHER. YOU TAUGHT ME WHAT A SUPPLY CHAIN IS. I DID NOT WANT TO KNOW, BUT I KNOW NOW, AND KNOWLEDGE IS A BURDEN I BEAR WITH GRACE." (Emberfall 18 — the handoff to Vane)

14.content:
Name: Sergeant Aldwin Crake
Age: Fifty-odd
Race: Human
Home Location: Fort Bramblehold (twenty-year posting); now The Dreadhold
Status: Alive. Commander of the Undergrowth's militia. Org chart title: MILITIA
Personality: Weary, dry, quietly moved by small decency, entirely unbothered by commanding the smallest, highest-stakes unit of his career
Physical Description: Gray beard, a broken nose (twice), an eyebrow scar stuck in permanent skepticism
Actions in Story: Defected at Millbrook, talked the Bramblehold garrison into surrendering, tallied gear before the Gray Zone expedition. On the road to Aurelium, commanded the small four-rider advance party (himself, Brandon, Clarissa, Sable) on a hard two-day push to Thornwall while Baron Wexley took the slower wagon train, then quietly orchestrated the takedown of the compromised inspectors holding Master Halloway, calling it, afterward, "a boring morning, gentlemen" to the dazed men in the street
Relationships: Twenty-year Bramblehold veteran, now Brandon's
Key Abilities/Ranks: Twenty years of soldiering. No rank beyond Sergeant, worn like it outranks everyone else anyway
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "Twenty years I've served Bramblehold. I'll not bleed for a double-pressed seal." (Emberfall 16 — defecting at Millbrook)
- "Twenty years I waited for orders worth following, Dread Lord. Never figured they'd come from a lad in a borrowed vest." (Emberfall 16 — after the fort falls)
- "My lord, you're the cargo. Precious, F-rank in combat, SS in consequences. Ride in the middle." (Emberfall 21 — march to Aurelium)

15.comment: Vane, Ser Dagric Vane, Dagric Vane
15.content:
Name: Ser Dagric Vane
Age: Adult, unspecified
Race: Human
Home Location: Fort Bramblehold; now The Dreadhold
Status: Alive. Knight, now sworn to the Undergrowth — and, as of recently, wielder of Gerald
Personality: Proud, image-conscious, developing real depth under the polish
Physical Description: Tall, oiled black hair, polished armor, silver-chased scabbard
Actions in Story: Lost a duel to Grondulf, yielded, swore fealty. In the Gray Zone, delivered mercy strokes to the Drinker's drained victims. Formally received Gerald from Clarissa in a small ceremony, taking on the sword's opinions along with its weight. Scouted ahead of the Aurelium-bound column, then rode atop Grondulf with Button through the night to reach Thornwall before midnight, leaving a tourney-style chalk bracket on the depot wall to brief the second group on the situation inside. Helped subdue the compromised inspectors and free Master Halloway
Relationships: Formerly Wexley's knight. Now carries Gerald and, with him, two centuries of opinions
Key Abilities/Ranks: B-Rank, tourney-trained. Now also responsible for a sentient sword's emotional wellbeing
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "Far enough, I think. Ser Dagric Vane, sworn blade of House Wexley, and you would be the boy the Reeve wet himself describing." (Emberfall 16 — the duel challenge)
- "Second at the Grand Melee. It relocated me. Like furniture." (Emberfall 16 — immediately after losing)
- "Name unknown, of Ashwick, taken uncounted. The Undergrowth counts you now." (Emberfall 18 — Gray Zone mercy rites)

16.content:
Name: Baron Aldous Wexley III, later "Baron Emeritus"
Age: Thirty-five
Race: Human
Home Location: Fort Bramblehold, specifically the suite with the south windows; currently leading the wagon train toward his mother's townhouse on Goldrow, Aurelium
Status: Alive. Former baron, now envoy, wagon-train commander, and — nervously — a man about to introduce his mother to reality
Personality: Cowardly by habit, genuinely brave in small increments when it counts. Increasingly self-aware, and increasingly trying to earn things rather than just inherit or fake them
Physical Description: Soft, doughy, wispy blond mustache, a doublet that's trying too hard
Actions in Story: Surrendered the fort, kept a title and a suite, turned his one asset (gossip) into real value for the Undergrowth. Revealed his mother, the Dowager Baroness Lady Ophelia Wexley (71), doesn't yet know the fort fell — and asked Brandon to accompany him to her townhouse on Goldrow, four days before the feast, to win her over in person. Called in a decades-old favor from Master Quillon, a Goldrow tailor who dressed his father and half the front tables at Halvard's feast, to outfit the whole capital-bound party on short notice. Has been developing a secret "gift protocol" idea for the crown presentation, convinced it's the one good idea three generations of Wexleys have produced. Now commands the slower wagon train (Gruk, Melvus, Fennick, three soldiers, the county's formal gift-manuscript, and Fennick's twine) while Brandon's group rides ahead
Relationships: Formerly lorded over Vane, Crake, Fenn. Son of Dowager Baroness Ophelia Wexley. Holds a favor owed by Master Quillon since 1241
Key Abilities/Ranks: None combat — his ancestral sword is decorative and may not physically come out of its scabbard. A genuinely impressive, and improving, network of gossip and social leverage
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "Wexleys do not yield! We WAIT!" (Emberfall 16 — hiding in the statue)
- "Wexleys don't work. Wexleys delegate. Father delegated. Grandfather delegated so hard he delegated the delegating." (Emberfall 16 — surrender negotiation)
- "She doesn't attend Saint Halvard's feast, Dread Lord. She operates it." (Emberfall 19 — revealing the Dowager Baroness)

17.comment: Fenn, Reeve Fenn, Ossric Fenn, the Reeve
17.content:
Name: Reeve Ossric Fenn
Age: Perhaps fifty
Race: Human
Home Location: Millbrook (tax office); currently in custody at The Dreadhold
Status: Disgraced and captured. Former tax collector, current cautionary tale
Personality: Craven, self-important, entirely unrepentant
Physical Description: Pear-shaped, waxed mustache, three chins, rings on six fingers he didn't earn
Actions in Story: Ran a double-sealed levy fraud in Millbrook, skimming on top of the Baron's own skimming. Blackmailed Tobbin into silence. Exposed publicly at Millbrook. Caught mid-escape trying to steal the fort's strongbox. Later investigation revealed his fraud was worse and smarter than one village: he ran a completely fabricated "salt tax" against Reedmarsh specifically, a copper per pound with no basis in any crown law, which cost the fishing village a third of its winter stores in one bad year and sent two families to the Millbrook poorhouse (one never returned). Also sold two-thirds of the county's entire winter grain reserve to an outside factor for cash, the same month he was skimming levies — the sale Tobbin and Clarissa are now working to claw back
Relationships: Blackmailed Tobbin. Served Wexley. Indirectly responsible for Reedmarsh's hardship, which shaped Mira Coldbrook's initial hostility toward any new lord
Key Abilities/Ranks: None. Corruption isn't a Guild rank, though it probably should be
Key Quotes: Fenn is only on-page during his exposure and capture at Millbrook, so these are different beats within that single arc rather than a true beginning/middle/end.
- "Where are the goats? Tobbin, where are the ANIMALS? This is a levy collection, not a town meeting!" (Emberfall 16 — arrival)
- "THE BARON WILL HEAR OF THIS! BRAMBLEHOLD HAS WALLS, BOY! WALLS!" (Emberfall 16 — mid-exposure)
- "(nobody look up… nobody look up… everyone is looking up…)" (Emberfall 16, internal thought — caught mid-tiptoe with the strongbox)

18.content:
Name: Tobbin
Age: Adult, unspecified
Race: Human
Home Location: Millbrook; now The Dreadhold Records office
Status: Alive. Master of Records for the Undergrowth. Org chart title: RECORDS
Personality: Meek turned steady the moment he was given real authority and a desk that doesn't wobble
Physical Description: Ink-stained fingers, thin, looks like he hasn't slept since spring — he hasn't
Actions in Story: Forced by Fenn to forge seals, kept secret copies, confessed publicly. The night his sister's mill deed was found in the fort's strongbox and quietly returned unasked, he walked to the Dreadhold gate himself and handed over five years of levy records for three counties, asking for the Master of Records job on the spot. Ran the county's first honest missing-persons audit (41 official vs. 341 real). First official act in the new job: tracking down "the wheat-and-weasel man," the grain factor (seal: a wheat sheaf and a weasel) who bought two-thirds of the county's stolen winter grain reserve from Fenn for cash in August — an ongoing recovery case Clarissa is pursuing with a drafted "recovery letter" that quotes the factor's own seal back at him next to the word FRAUD. Drafted the Undergrowth's formal gift-manuscript for the crown. Left holding the ledgers and the ongoing fraud prosecutions while Brandon's party is away
Relationships: Nessa's brother. Formerly Fenn's hostage-employee
Key Abilities/Ranks: Meticulous record-keeping, born of necessity
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "I have COPIES, I kept COPIES, they're sewn inside my mattress like a coward!" (Emberfall 16 — confession)
- "Master of Records for the Undergrowth. I accept. I'll want a room with a lock and a desk that doesn't wobble." (Emberfall 16 — same evening, taking the job)
- "Find the wheat-and-weasel man's other buyers, every county he's sold into this year. If he skimmed us, he's skimmed someone else, and that's leverage on top of leverage." (Emberfall 16-17 — first act as Master of Records)

19.content:
Name: Nessa
Age: Adult, unspecified
Race: Human
Home Location: Millbrook (the mill); currently also sheltering twelve refugee families from Mosshaven there
Status: Alive. Runs the only working mill for eight miles
Personality: Guarded, exacting, softens slowly when actually listened to instead of talked at
Physical Description: Flour-dusted, arms folded, patience thin
Actions in Story: Negotiated a fair grinding rate with Brandon. Revealed her mother was from Mosshaven and that all twelve displaced families sheltered in her millhouse have now gone home to a blessed well and a sapling learning their names. Walked from her millhouse at dawn on departure day specifically to see the column off, and handed Brandon a travel bundle of bread, cheese, and dried chamomile — dryly noting that actual flowers have started growing out of the Dreadhold's hallway floor, a fact the whole county is apparently discussing, and that she's chosen not to ask questions and simply dry what blooms
Relationships: Tobbin's sister
Key Abilities/Ranks: None combat. Runs a business better than most nobles run a county
Key Quotes (spread across the story):
- "The Wexleys took a fifth of every sack milled. A fifth, for the privilege of the crown's protection, which protected nothing, twice." (Emberfall 16 — negotiating terms)
- "My mother was from Mosshaven. Twelve families, Dread Lord. That's not a grievance. That's twelve families waiting to hear if they have a home to go back to." (Emberfall 17 — Grand County Council)
- "For the road. The flowers grow out of your hallway floor, Dread Lord. Whole county's talking. I don't ask questions. I just dry what blooms. Bring them all home." (Emberfall 21 — departure)

20.content:
Name: Assessor Wimble
Age: Roughly forty
Race: Human
Home Location: A regional Guild outpost (exact hall unspecified)
Status: Alive. Guild rank appraiser, freshly licensed and regretting it
Personality: Nervous, dutiful, thrilled beyond words to have survived the assignment
Physical Description: Small, round, ink-stained cuffs, green-and-gold Guild attire
Actions in Story: Sent to formally rank Brandon. Issued the finished Guild card: DARTH BRANDON, CLASS DREAD LORD, RANK A (ASCENDING), ATTRIBUTES REDACTED BY GUILD SAFETY ORDER, SEVEN DEEDS ON FILE, countersigned by Clarissa (S) and Vane (B) — the first Dread Lord registered since one "Vess of the Weeping Tower." Fled the fort immediately after, only for Brandon to remember the parting gift and send Sir Frederick sprinting after him at full voidling speed, resulting in the assessor's horse achieving a gait "previously unknown to the county" before Frederick sat down in the road and gifted him a fork, a damp button, some coins, and a wedge of cheese
Relationships: Countersigned by Clarissa and Vane
Key Abilities/Ranks: Newly licensed. Barely
Key Quotes: Wimble is only on-page for one visit (Emberfall 16), so these are three different beats within that single day rather than a true spread.
- "Please don't feed me to anything." (arrival)
- "I'm going to be in a footnote." (mid-assessment, discovering Section Nine)
- "Some people run from a redacted card. Some people take it as a challenge." (departure)

21.content:
Name: Hobb
Age: Young — the youngest of Crake's defectors
Race: Human
Home Location: Fort Bramblehold; mother runs a pig farm in an unnamed village
Status: Alive. Soldier, now Marta's second-in-command for sewing, which nobody saw coming, including him
Personality: Eager, easily flustered, unexpectedly skilled with a needle
Physical Description: Blushes on command, fumbles his own helmet
Actions in Story: Defected with Crake. Got promoted for a dead-straight hemming stitch. Blew the surrender horn — described as sounding "like a goose being audited," and somehow exactly right for the occasion
Relationships: Part of Crake's unit; mother runs a pig farm
Key Abilities/Ranks: A very good hemming stitch. No combat rank of note
Key Quotes: Hobb has almost no direct dialogue — mostly internal panic and narrated action (defecting, the hemming stitch, blowing the surrender horn) — so there isn't real spread to draw from.
- "(joined a cult before lunch… mum's going to have OPINIONS… best job I've ever had…)" (Emberfall 16, internal thought)

22.content:
Name: Widow Pell
Age: Older; a widow
Race: Human
Home Location: Unspecified village — one of eleven represented at the Dreadhold grievance council
Status: Alive. One half of a decade-long pig feud
Personality: Proud, prickly, more ashamed than angry once you scratch the surface
Physical Description: Chapped hands, crooked shawl, grey hair losing the fight against its own knot
Actions in Story: Brought the feud to Brandon's court. Admitted under compulsion the fight was never about the pig — it was pride, and a fence she let rot rather than ask for help. Her reconciliation with Greta became the template for Brandon's later "honesty experiment" (wine mixed with cider, since there's no citrus for three hundred miles) at the wider county grievance council
Relationships: Estranged, then reconciled, with Greta
Key Abilities/Ranks: None
Key Quotes: Widow Pell only appears during the Grand County Council (Emberfall 17), so these are the arc's three beats — opening anger, the turn, the confession — rather than a story-wide spread.
- "That sow was MY grandmother's before she ever set foot in your village!" (opening)
- "You're the one they say talks men into kneeling. Fine. Talk HER into admitting whose sow it was, and I'll believe every word after." (mid-confrontation)
- "I never once came to collect her because I was ashamed, Greta, not because I forgot." (the confession)

23.content:
Name: Greta
Age: Adult, unspecified
Race: Human
Home Location: Unspecified village — rival to Widow Pell's
Status: Alive. The other half of the pig feud
Personality: Guarded, practical, more sentimental than she lets on
Physical Description: Broad-shouldered, burn scar on one forearm, dark hair pulled back tight
Actions in Story: Took in and fed the disputed pig through two hard winters. Admitted under compulsion the fight was really about being hungry once
Relationships: Estranged, then reconciled, with Widow Pell
Key Abilities/Ranks: None
Key Quotes: Greta only appears during the Grand County Council (Emberfall 17), so these are that single arc's beats.
- "The sow wandered onto our land in '31, simple as that." (opening defense)
- "Because we were hungry that year too, and taking her in fed six mouths that mattered more to me than whose brand was on her ear." (the confession)

24.content:
Name: Elder Bramwick
Age: Elderly
Race: Human
Home Location: Coldwater Hollow
Status: Alive. Village elder of Coldwater Hollow, professionally tired of everyone's grudges
Personality: Wry, long-suffering, sharper than his bluster suggests
Physical Description: Walking stick with chains, bad hip, beard yellowed at the chin
Actions in Story: Brought eleven years of boundary disputes to Dreadhold to settle before winter. Cracked the pig feud in one line. Revealed the miasma has crossed his south pasture line and killed two goats that "didn't die proper," which turned a village grievance into a military problem. Traded quarry access for Brandon clearing the miasma. Locked in a running, decades-old dispute with Mira Coldbrook over fishing rights (his grandfather allegedly sold her mother the eddy rights for "a short-poured cask of winter ale"), eventually resolved by Brandon into a real leased split — Reedmarsh keeps the water, Coldwater Hollow gets a cut for bank access
Relationships: Represents Coldwater Hollow. Long, prickly, ultimately fond rivalry with Mira Coldbrook
Key Abilities/Ranks: None
Key Quotes: Bramwick recurs across the Grand County Council and the later fishing-rights dispute with Mira, giving a small but real spread.
- "Heard you settled a fort without drawing a blade, boy. Curious to see you settle a pig." (Emberfall 17 — arrival)
- "Sounds to me the pig was never the argument at all." (Emberfall 17 — resolving the feud)
- "THE CASK WAS SHORT-POURED!" (Emberfall 17-18 — the ongoing feud with Mira over fishing rights)

25.content:
Name: Mira Coldbrook
Age: Adult, unspecified; a grandmother
Race: Human
Home Location: Reedmarsh
Status: Alive. Reedmarsh fisherwoman, newly appointed Captain of the Undergrowth's navy. Org chart title: NAVY
Personality: Blunt, competent, warming slowly to the absurdity of her own new rank — and to Brandon himself, after arriving deeply skeptical
Physical Description: Net-scarred forearms, the flat unimpressed stare of someone who has heard every excuse a fish or a nobleman has ever offered
Actions in Story: Identified the unused Coldwater river route, accepted command of the Undergrowth's fledgling navy, settled a generations-old fishing dispute with Elder Bramwick into an actual lease. Arrived at the Grand County Council openly skeptical, backed by her own clerk — a thin, reedy-voiced man also named Pell (no relation to Widow Pell of the pig feud) — who exposed a second Fenn fraud on her behalf: a completely fabricated "salt tax" that cost Reedmarsh dearly, revealed in full when her grandson Tam blurted the details before anyone gave him permission to speak. Warmed considerably once Brandon confirmed the fraud on sight rather than stalling
Relationships: Grandmother to Tam. Employs a clerk named Pell (distinct from Widow Pell). Old rival, now reluctant business partner, of Elder Bramwick
Key Abilities/Ranks: Riverine logistics and navigation; now a naval commission she's still getting used to saying out loud
Key Quotes (small spread across her introduction and the fraud reveal):
- "Reedmarsh." (Emberfall 17 — arrival, announced to no one in particular)
- "My mother fished those eddies because your grandfather SOLD her the rights for a cask of winter ale, Bramwick, and YOU know it." (Emberfall 17-18 — fishing rights dispute)
- "No, love. That's the terrifying part." (Emberfall 17 — to Tam, after Brandon's "looting rival kingdoms" joke)

26.content:
Name: Tam
Age: Twelve
Race: Human
Home Location: Reedmarsh
Status: Alive. Mira's grandson, newest and loudest member of a two-person navy fan club
Personality: Twelve and thrilled about literally everything right now, prone to blurting the truth before anyone gives him permission to speak — which turned out to matter
Physical Description: Dried-fish sack permanently over one shoulder, wrists jutting from sleeves he's outgrown
Actions in Story: Present when Brandon appointed his grandmother Captain of the Undergrowth's navy, and has not stopped saying the word "navy" since. At the Grand County Council, spoke out of turn to explain the fabricated "salt tax" in three blunt words — "Fenn made it up" — before Mira's own clerk could finish his careful pitch, cutting straight to why Reedmarsh lost a third of its winter stores. Cannot currently swim; learning to is now a term of the Coldwater Hollow fishing lease
Relationships: Mira's grandson
Key Abilities/Ranks: None yet. Enthusiasm and, apparently, better instincts for cutting to the point than the adults around him
Key Quotes: Tam appears only at the Grand County Council and the navy appointment (Emberfall 17-18), both close together.
- "Fenn made it up. Two years ago… Nobody in Reedmarsh can read the seal, sir. That's the trick of it." (Emberfall 17 — exposing the salt tax)
- "GRAN. GRAN. WE'RE A NAVY." (Emberfall 18)

27.content:
Name: Edda of Fenhollow
Age: Around sixty, by her own account of "forty years" of herb-craft plus apprenticeship
Race: Human
Home Location: Fenhollow
Status: Alive. Healer, now functionally attached to Brandon's inner circle
Personality: Quiet, unhurried, has clearly done this kind of grim work before and doesn't flinch from it
Physical Description: Small, root-brown, unhurried hands — the hands of someone who has closed a lot of eyes
Actions in Story: Volunteered for the Gray Zone expedition, correctly diagnosed the miasma's victims, administered the tincture, and personally compiled the real 341-name death toll of the Drinker's thirty-year hunt on birch bark. Is now touring every affected village with that list, reading the names aloud one by one as a form of memorial — the reading began at Mosshaven, under the silver sapling, the day the party departed for Aurelium
Relationships: Represents Fenhollow. Has appointed herself Brandon's unofficial minder. Frequently seated beside Gruk, for reasons everyone else has noticed and she hasn't fully addressed either
Key Abilities/Ranks: Decades of herb-craft and battlefield triage; no Guild rank given
Key Quotes (spread across her introduction, the expedition, and the aftermath):
- "You've got a monster's seed in your pocket, Dread Lord. I'd not plant that near anything you love until we know what grows." (Emberfall 17-18 — before the expedition)
- "He was drunk from. Like a cup." (Emberfall 18 — diagnosing the Drinker's victims)
- "Aether-rich water. Brewers would kill for it. Alchemists would kill brewers for it." (Emberfall 18 — planning Mosshaven's future)

28.content:
Name: The Drinker, later "the Sapling"
Age: Fell through a tapestry hole into this world thirty years ago
Race: Unknown. A being from outside the tapestry, like Brandon and Clarissa, who "landed wrong." Its true form was a miasma-shrouded mire the size of a farmhouse
Home Location: The mire (Gray Zone); now rooted at Mosshaven, planted over the exact plot where it once burned a man's home
Status: Formerly a 30-year serial predator. Now a small, newly sentient sapling, apparently teaching itself hospitality
Personality: Was performative and deeply lonely. Now small, shy, relieved — and, per Gruk and returning villagers, quietly eager to learn every name it's told
Physical Description (as the Drinker): A heap of wet, breathing petals the size of a farmhouse. As the Sapling: silver bark, translucent violet-veined leaves, warm "like a sleeping animal"
Actions in Story: Drained 341 people over thirty years, kept 106 alive as a dormant hoard, took two of Brandon's memories as payment before he forced their return via Silver Tongue. Revealed it once fought a "golden nothing-thing" with a "singing sword" thirty years ago — the man who became King Aurelian — and ate the heaviest parts of his memory as payment, which is why the court found him pliable enough to crown within two years. Mercy-killed by Gerald at Brandon's command; the killing blow freed it instead, releasing its 106 held souls and leaving behind a seed. Now stands at Mosshaven watching villagers return, learning names one by one, apparently by design — Gruk has taken to watering it and reciting names to it, unofficially becoming its "Guestbook Man." Edda's formal reading of its 341 victims' names began here
Relationships: A fellow tapestry "hole" like Brandon and Clarissa. Directly responsible for King Aurelian's amnesia. Unknowingly raised the hatchling who became Button Firewood inside its own body
Key Abilities/Ranks: (Formerly) memory-draining, possession of "vessels," near-unkillable by conventional means. Now: none — it's a tree, and apparently a good listener
Key Quotes (spread across the single-day confrontation's opening, climax, and aftermath):
- "I do not eat because I hunger. I eat because I am drowning, and minds are the only thing that floats." (Emberfall 18 — early in the negotiation)
- "You, the bright one, and me… Ask your soldiers, when you return, how long the mire has been here. They will say forever. They are wrong by exactly thirty years." (Emberfall 18 — the reveal)
- "Oh. Roots. I have — I had forgotten what it was to be held by something instead of holding." (Emberfall 18 — as the Sapling, immediately after)

29.content:
Name: Corvin Ashe
Age: Unstated; twenty-two years into his service
Race: Human
Home Location: Aurelium (the capital)
Status: Alive. Royal Herald, Third Class, in service to King Aurelian
Personality: Professionally neutral for twenty-two years running — cracking, slightly, only around Brandon
Physical Description: Tidy, gray at the temples, aligns his teacup with the grain of the table without seeming to notice he's doing it
Actions in Story: Delivered the King's formal message to Brandon under a fidelity geas — a spell that forces him to recite messages exactly as given and forbids him from remembering the words afterward, though he retains the tone and circumstances. Delivered a two-part message: the crown's official non-contest of Brandon's new barony, and a private, desperate plea from the King himself, sent with no escort and no clerks, saying he no longer trusts his own court. Carried Brandon's blunt, half-joking reply back the same way. Broke his own twenty-two-year professional neutrality twice in one conversation — first to laugh, genuinely, at Brandon's audacity, and second to offer unsolicited, off-the-record advice: bring someone who knows the fish fork, because the court forgives monsters before bad manners
Relationships: In service to King Aurelian. Delivered both Clarissa's original summoning orders and now Brandon's capital invitation — a professional, not personal, throughline between the two plot threads
Key Abilities/Ranks: Bound by a fidelity geas as a licensed Third Class Herald — cannot misquote, embellish, or withhold a formally received message
Key Quotes (his one visit, arrival through departure):
- "I don't know what that means. I never know what the messages mean. It's a mercy of the job." (arrival)
- "Kings who bypass their own chancellery and seal messages at midnight are kings who no longer trust the machinery around them." (mid-visit, breaking neutrality)
- "The court forgives monsters before it forgives bad table manners." (departure, off-the-record advice)

30.content:
Name: Aurelian the First, King of Flameworth, "Chosen of the Golden Summons"
Age: Has reigned thirty years
Race: Human — and, per the Drinker, another tapestry "hole," a Chosen from outside this world originally
Home Location: Aurelium, the royal palace (the capital, which he renamed after himself roughly thirty years ago)
Status: Alive. Reigning King. Increasingly a puppet of his own court
Personality: Hollowed-out, frightened, uncharacteristically direct in the one private message he's sent unfiltered by his court
Physical Description: Not physically described yet on page — known so far only through secondhand accounts (Clarissa's, Vane's) as a man who looks "hollow," "like reading someone else's mail," and who wears "the biggest crown" Clarissa has ever seen
Actions in Story: Originally arrived in Flameworth as a Chosen thirty years ago and defeated the Drinker in an early, failed confrontation — the Drinker took his horse and his memory of his own mother's face as payment. Because he arrived with "no past," the court found him useful and crowned him within two years. Has spent thirty years increasingly memory-holed and sidelined by his own Chancellor and court, signing orders — including the one that summoned and aimed Clarissa at Brandon — without reading them, because "the ones who wrote it have been writing my orders for years." Recently began dreaming of a crosswalk (an actual memory trying to resurface, per Pemberly, timed suspiciously close to the Drinker's death). Sent Corvin Ashe alone, with no escort, to privately warn Brandon that someone in his own court witnessed Brandon's arrival from Earth, and to invite him to the Feast of Saint Halvard as an open guest — specifically so his court "cannot touch him quietly"
Relationships: Employer, in effect, of Clarissa's original summoning. Secretly appealing to Brandon for help against his own court. Possibly connected to the Drinker/Brandon/Clarissa tapestry-tear pattern as an earlier, fourth case
Key Abilities/Ranks: Unknown directly; was a Chosen once, details unrevealed
Key Quotes: King Aurelian has not appeared on-page directly — every line is secondhand, via Vane's and Clarissa's accounts, or his one written message.
- "I have been King thirty years, and for thirty years there has been a hole in me where a life should be." (his private message, Emberfall 19-20)
- "Someone at my court watched you cross between worlds. It was not me. I no longer trust the men who see such things and say nothing." (same message)
- "Come to the capital before the coronation feast of Saint Halvard — come as a guest, openly, where they cannot touch you quietly." (same message, the invitation itself)

31.content:
Name: The Prophecy (unauthorized, per Pemberly)
Origin: Delivered by Yminia to Brandon in a dream on Emberfall 14, Year 1247 — technically against policy, since Chosen aren't supposed to get a prophecy before their first full week
Full Text: "WHEN THE HOLLOW WEARS ITS TWENTIETH NAME, A LIGHT SHALL SHINE FROM THE WEST THAT IS NOT THE SUN. A WITNESS RIDES BEHIND IT, ONE WHO SAW YOU CROSS. THE FORT OF THE THIRD SHALL KNEEL OR CRACK, AND THE DEVOURER'S TABLE SHALL SEAT ELEVEN, THEN TWELVE, THEN ONE TOO MANY."
Status: Mostly fulfilled, one clause reinterpreted
Known Mappings:
- "the hollow wears its twentieth name" — Button Firewood's twentieth, permanent name. Fulfilled
- "a light shall shine from the west" — Clarissa, sent from Flameworth in the west. Fulfilled
- "a witness rides behind it, one who saw you cross" — reinterpreted. Clarissa herself confirms she never saw anything "cross" — she saw a crosswalk, then a windshield, then woke up dead. Someone else in King Aurelian's own court appears to have witnessed the actual crossing, since a royal herald arrived asking for Brandon by his real surname, which nobody in the county knew. Unresolved and increasingly central to the capital plot
- "the fort of the third shall kneel or crack" — Baron Aldous Wexley THE THIRD, Fort Bramblehold. Fulfilled
- "the Devourer's table shall seat eleven, then twelve, then one too many" — the cult's headcount, eleven before Brandon and twelve after. "One too many" still unresolved — watch for a betrayal, overcrowding, or unwelcome addition
Complications: Pemberly believes Yminia may have "forgotten something on purpose" regarding the witness. Separately, Pemberly has identified three confirmed tapestry tears (Brandon, Clarissa, the Drinker) and suspects a possible fourth in King Aurelian himself
Key Quotes:
- "'Dread Lord Brandon of the Undergrowth? Delivery from the Thornwall Assessor's Office. Instructions were: your hand, no other, before you reach Thornwall.'" (Emberfall 21 — the crossroads interception)
- "Chancellery inspectors. Two days ahead of us. On our road." (Emberfall 21 — mid-journey)
- "Halloway paid my dispatch out of his own purse. Guild rule one: debts ride both ways." (Emberfall 22 — Thornwall, committing fully)

32.content:
Name: Three Tears in the Weave (ongoing mystery)
Summary: Summonings into Magisa are supposed to be controlled "doors" run by the court's own rituals. In truth, per the Tailor's own confession, ALL tapestry tears — Brandon, Clarissa, and the Drinker (and likely King Aurelian) — are his direct work, not the court's. He tears holes into other realities to pull in "replacement thread" for a four-thousand-year-old Tapestry that is fraying faster than he can patch it, and he personally orchestrated the crosswalk accident that killed both Brandon and Clarissa in a single cut, calling it "triage"
Key Evidence: The Tailor confessed everything directly to Brandon at Thornwall. Yminia's own loom shows three holes and she visibly recognized the Tailor through it, going "very cold" — suggesting a history or a deliberately suppressed memory. Most importantly: the Tailor himself claims his "clean cut" on Brandon and Clarissa was tampered with afterward by an unknown third party — something braided Brandon's thread into "load-bearing" importance and caused Clarissa, a Hero, to defect from her assigned role, which "the pattern does not allow." The Tailor denies being Yminia's prophesied "witness who saw you cross" and seems genuinely troubled that something is operating in his own seams without his knowledge
Stakes: There are now at least two unaccounted-for cosmic actors: the Tailor (identified, semi-cooperative, resource-starved) and the mysterious third party who altered his work on Brandon and Clarissa after the fact. King Aurelian remains a likely fourth tapestry tear, caused by the same "sloppy" early leak that became the Drinker
Key Quotes (arrival, hostage ordeal, aftermath):
- "The wrist is theater. My honor's more bruised than my body, and my honor's been bruised since the palace stopped reading my reports in '39." (Emberfall 22 — on being freed)
- "They fed me twice, called me 'Master' throughout, and threatened my pension rather than my person. Professional men." (Emberfall 22 — describing the ordeal)
- "SS outcomes, F everything else… the discrepancy walks and TALKS." (Emberfall 22, internal thought — meeting Brandon in person)

33.comment: The Feast of Saint Halvard / Capital Summons
33.content:
Name: The Feast of Saint Halvard (current plot thread)
Summary: Flameworth's largest annual formal gathering — every noble house, church bishops, and Guild assessors attend, held in Aurelium
Trigger: King Aurelian's private, unescorted plea via Herald Corvin Ashe, inviting Brandon as an open guest specifically so his own court "cannot touch him quietly." Brandon's reply back all but confirmed he suspects a coup
Complications piling up: Brandon's Guild card finalized at OUTCOME RATING SS / COMBAT RATING F, a discrepancy Master Halloway compares to readings once associated with the Demon Ruler — showing that card at the palace gate scanner could reclassify Brandon on the spot as a kingdom-level threat inside the palace walls, which "men in the Chancellery would find convenient." Four fake Chancellery inspectors already tried to intercept and forge a replacement card at Thornwall before being stopped. A reality-bending entity calling itself the Tailor has personally warned Brandon that "the thing in the court" will find him regardless. Baron Wexley's mother, Dowager Baroness Ophelia — the single most connected woman in the capital — doesn't yet know the fort fell, and must be won over in a private visit four days before the feast or risk publicly destroying the Undergrowth's credibility out of family reflex. Every attending house must present a tribute to the crown, announced to all five hundred nobles in the hall; the Baron has a secret "gift protocol" idea for the Undergrowth's presentation that he's convinced is the one good idea three generations of Wexleys have produced
Timeline: Five days' travel to Aurelium at wagon pace; the advance party made Thornwall in roughly a day and a half by splitting the column
Status: Currently en route. The advance party (Brandon, Crake, Clarissa, Vane, Button, Grondulf, Sable) has cleared Thornwall and the Tailor incident; the slower wagon train (Baron Wexley, Gruk, Melvus, Fennick, three soldiers) is still on the road
Key Quotes (opening, revelation, closing):
- "I've been reading forged reports about you for a week, and the forgeries are more modest than the truth. That's new. They usually exaggerate." (opening)
- "I am the Tailor. Not a god. I am what the loom uses when it needs new thread and is too proud to ask." (the confession)
- "I'll be watching. I'm always watching. It's the one part of the job I've never once botched." (parting warning)

34.content:
Name: The Undergrowth's Governing Structure
Summary: What started as a failed cult has become a functioning (if bizarre) county government, tracked on a real organizational chart Clarissa keeps updating in chalk
Current Roster:
- DREAD LORD — Brandon
- PROPERTY — Grondulf (fort and infrastructure)
- RECORDS — Tobbin (levies, fraud evidence, missing-persons audits)
- AUDIT / logistics — Clarissa
- MILITIA — Sergeant Crake
- NAVY — Mira Coldbrook (four vessels, twelve crew, and a grandson who can't swim)
- Envoy — Baron Wexley (noble gossip and connections)
- Unofficial: Edda (healer/advisor), Melvus (chronicler/propagandist), Vane (knight, mercy-rites, capital knowledge)
Notable Assets Recently Acquired: a river trade route (Mira's), a quarry (traded for miasma clearance with Coldwater Hollow), a revived village and aether-rich well at Mosshaven, and a rapidly growing reputation as the only local government that asks about winter before being shouted at about it
Key Quotes:
- "Nobody in this county eats turnips on my watch either." — Clarissa
- "Captain Coldbrook of the Undergrowth Royal Navy. Commanding four vessels, twelve crew, and a grandson who can't swim." — Mira

35.content:
Name: Sable
Age: Young adult, unspecified
Race: Human
Home Location: Thornwall (Guild posting)
Status: Alive. Guild Courier, Second Class; now informally attached to Brandon's advance party
Personality: Professional, dryly self-aware, increasingly resigned to being swept into a conspiracy she didn't sign up for
Physical Description: Short-cropped hair, road leathers, Guild badge at the collar
Actions in Story: Intercepted Brandon's column at a crossroads to deliver a sealed Guild parcel containing his finalized Guild card and a coded verbal warning from Master Halloway not to show it at the palace gate. Revealed Chancellery "inspectors" had been asking pointed questions at the Thornwall depot before her dispatch, and that Halloway sent her out the back gate at midnight, off the books, paid from his own purse. Accepted Brandon's invitation to travel with the column rather than ride back alone. Guided the advance party to a hayfield with a sightline on Halloway's office lamp, and climbed back through the office's bad-latch window — the same one she's used twice now to check on him
Relationships: Loyal to Master Halloway, who's clearly mentored or protected her professionally. Increasingly, reluctantly, one of Brandon's people
Key Abilities/Ranks: Guild Courier, Second Class. Knows Thornwall's roads and back routes better than anyone in the party
Key Quotes (referenced across the Baron's account, not yet met on-page):
- "She doesn't attend Saint Halvard's feast, Dread Lord. She operates it." — Baron Wexley
- "There is no middle ground with Mother. She arrives as our siege engine or our siege." — Baron Wexley
- "She takes it with lemon. No milk. Refuse a second cup and it's over." — Baron Wexley

36.content:
Name: Master Halloway
Age: Unstated; referenced serving since at least 1239 ("the palace stopped reading my reports in '39")
Race: Human
Home Location: Thornwall — the Guild's side office on River Street
Status: Alive. Guild Assessor, operator of Thornwall's independent side office
Personality: Dry, precise, quietly defiant of the palace bureaucracy that sidelined him decades ago
Physical Description: Thin, gray, ink-stained fingers, a bandaged wrist he's keeping injured on purpose
Actions in Story: Ran the scrying sphere that finalized Brandon's true Guild card — SS Outcome Rating, F Combat Rating, a discrepancy he'd only seen the instrument register once before in forty years of service. Sent Sable out at midnight, off the books and on his own coin, to get the card to Brandon before the palace could intercept it. Held hostage overnight by four fake "Chancellery inspectors" who forced him to forge replacement cards; deliberately botched two of the forgeries and kept his wrist "slow" as cover. Freed when Brandon's party stormed the office
Relationships: Protective of Sable. Openly distrustful of the Chancellery. The one Guild assessor, per Sable, "the palace never bought"
Key Abilities/Ranks: Senior Guild Assessor, operates the county's scrying sphere
Key Quotes:
- "The wrist is theater. My honor's more bruised than my body, and my honor's been bruised since the palace stopped reading my reports in '39."
- "They fed me twice, called me 'Master' throughout, and threatened my pension rather than my person. Professional men."
- "Slowest wrist in the Guild."

37.content:
Name: "The Tailor" (self-given title; true name unknown)
Age/Race: Unknown. Not a god ("gods are woven things, decorative selvage") — by his own account, an entity from entirely outside the Tapestry, older than or parallel to the pantheon. Appears human-shaped in a purple sash, but his shadow lags half a second behind his movements and the sash's color "sits on it like frost on glass" rather than being dyed
Home Location: Unknown — moves through gaps in space "not wide enough for a man"
Status: Active. Self-professed maintainer of the Tapestry, undecided about what to do regarding Brandon
Personality: Weary, overworked, and increasingly candid once pressed — reads less like a villain and more like an understaffed contractor four thousand years behind on a job nobody will fund
Physical Description: Pleasant, deliberately forgettable face that eyes slide off; a lagging shadow; a frost-like purple sash
Actions in Story: Froze six minutes of time at Thornwall to speak with Brandon privately, offering one truthful, cost-free answer to any question ("the Drinker offered you a table. I'm offering you a window"). Revealed, under direct questioning, that he is THE TAILOR — the entity that "patches" Yminia's fraying, four-thousand-year-old Tapestry by tearing holes into other realities and pulling in replacement thread when stories there have effectively ended. Confirmed he personally caused the crosswalk accident that killed both Brandon and Clarissa, calling it "triage, not cruelty" — harvesting two used-up Earth lives (a truck driver and a desk worker) in a single cut to patch the weave. Thirty years ago he pulled a "golden thread" through and lost his grip, and the resulting leak became the Drinker. Under a second question ("Why?"), Brandon's Silver Tongue compulsion visibly forced an answer out of him — which should be impossible, by his own admission, since he claims not to be part of the weave the compulsion operates on. This led to his biggest revelation: something else, a third party, has been operating in his seams — braiding Brandon's "spare thread" into something load-bearing, and causing Clarissa (a Hero, who by the pattern's own rules "do not defect") to break from her assigned role. He denies being the "witness who saw you cross" from Yminia's prophecy, and seems genuinely unsettled that someone else is interfering with his work. Ultimately backed off after Brandon declared himself "a guest of the king," released the compelled inspectors, and vanished
Relationships: Personally, directly responsible for Brandon's and Clarissa's deaths and summonings. Created the tapestry tear that became the Drinker. Yminia recognized him on sight through her loom and went "very cold," suggesting a history or a suppressed memory involving him specifically. Aware of, and apparently as much in the dark about, a mysterious third party manipulating events from outside even his own operation
Key Abilities/Ranks: Can freeze time in a localized area. Can compel or "borrow" a person's limb. Can move through solid matter. Tears holes between realities as his fundamental function — this is "always his," not the court's summoning rituals, which he considers self-important theater. Notably NOT immune to Brandon's Silver Tongue, which surprised even him
Key Quotes:
- "I am the Tailor. Not a god. I am what the loom uses when it needs new thread and is too proud to ask."
- "The truck. Mine. Two ended stories, one intersection… Nobody was going to miss either of you. That is not cruelty, that is triage."
- "A third party is operating in MY seams, Brandon Kelly. Your witness prophecy. Your 'saw you cross.' She thinks it was me. I know it was not."
- "I unpick threads, Brandon Kelly, and you keep re-weaving them faster than I pull. It's genuinely irritating. I came all this way to see the loom."
- "Kill you? Brandon. I already did. It didn't take. That is precisely my problem with you."

38.content:
Name: Lady Ophelia Wexley, Dowager Baroness
Age: Seventy-one
Race: Human
Home Location: A townhouse on Goldrow, Aurelium — "three doors from Quillon's"
Status: Alive. Not yet met on-page. Baron Wexley's mother, described as the single most connected information broker in the capital
Personality: By her son's account — sharp as a filleting knife, loyal to the Wexley name rather than to any individual Wexley, dangerous when the family is embarrassed, capable of ending a man's public life with one sentence
Physical Description: Not yet described on-page
Actions in Story: Referenced only so far. Does not attend Saint Halvard's feast — she runs its social machinery from the front tables, forty years running, and seated half the Privy Council through whispering campaigns. Does not yet know her son lost Fort Bramblehold; his letters have contained nothing but weather observations and lies for a season. The Baron and Brandon plan to visit her townhouse four days before the feast to win her over in person before a herald tells her cold — framed by the Baron as "she arrives as our siege engine or our siege." Flagged by Pemberly as a possible unwitting thread in the Chancellery conspiracy, due to thirty years of unexplained annual gift correspondence with the Lord Chancellor
Relationships: Mother of Baron Aldous Wexley III. Longtime, unexplained "account" with the Lord Chancellor. Responsible, indirectly, for exiling a Wexley cousin (Edmund) into foreign clergy after one embarrassing public incident
Key Abilities/Ranks: None combat. Forty years of accumulated social leverage across the capital's noble houses
Key Quotes (about her, not yet by her):
- "She doesn't attend Saint Halvard's feast, Dread Lord. She operates it." — Baron Wexley
- "There is no middle ground with Mother. She arrives as our siege engine or our siege." — Baron Wexley
- "She takes it with lemon. No milk. Refuse a second cup and it's over." — Baron Wexley

39.content:
Name: The Chancellery Conspiracy (developing)
Summary: A pattern of evidence suggesting the plot against Brandon — and possibly against King Aurelian himself — runs through the Lord Chancellor's office
Key Evidence: Four men in Chancellery purple sashes intercepted Brandon's Guild assessment at Thornwall. Two of them, Sorro and Merl, turned out to be ordinary Guild inspectors sent under false orders with edited memories, compelled into the job by the Tailor and left disoriented once released. Master Halloway was held hostage overnight and forced to forge a false version of Brandon's Guild card meant to reach the palace gate instead of the true SS/F rating. The Lord Chancellor has sent Dowager Baroness Ophelia Wexley a personal gift every year for as long as her son can remember — an "account," not a friendship, of unknown currency. The Lord Chancellor is also the man who has been drafting King Aurelian's orders for years, including the one that summoned and aimed Clarissa at Brandon, and who stood beside the palace's summoning chamber floor with its dozens of unexplained old scorch rings
Key Figures: The Lord Chancellor of Flameworth (not yet met on-page), the entity calling itself "the Tailor" (who may be managing or exploiting this conspiracy rather than leading it), Sorro and Merl (compromised Guild inspectors, memories restored but disoriented)
Status: Unconfirmed but actively suspected by Pemberly, Clarissa, and Brandon. The planned visit to the Dowager Baroness is now considered, per Pemberly, "an operation"
Key Quotes:
- "The Chancellery is pulling traffic records on us before we've even arrived… which means the gate check at Aurelium won't be routine, it'll be a trap with paperwork." — Clarissa
- "Lord Chancellor: thirty years drafting the King's orders, standing beside a summoning floor with dozens of rings… and a four-decade paid account with the most connected woman in Aurelium… flagging the tea. The tea is now an OPERATION." — Pemberly

40.content:
Name: The Summoning of Darth Brandon (origin event)
Summary: The story's opening event. Brandon dies on Earth crossing a marked crosswalk when a distracted driver runs a red light, and is offered a jokey four-option isekai menu by a disembodied "system" voice: (1) the royal palace with a princess and knights, (2) an abandoned dungeon full of cultists and monsters, (3) the wilderness for no reason, (4) somewhere random. He picks option two on pure instinct
Key Events: Lands mid-ritual in the Crypt of Saint Mildew, hailed on sight as "the Devourer of Realms" by a failed doomsday cult who'd been chanting for what they call a thousand years (actually about fifteen months) and clearly expected something more intimidating than a teenager in glasses and sneakers. The cult demonstrates their genuinely pathetic powers (Reek Cloud, Mildly Warm Hands, a Dread Visage that mostly sets its user's sleeve on fire) to earn his mercy. The ritual's tremors accidentally wake Grondulf, the dungeon's actual resident troll, who is owed fifteen months of back rent by the cult and considers the summoning itself a lease violation
Significance: Everything traces back to this one flippant menu choice — the Undergrowth, the entire cast, the whole plot
Key Quotes:
- "Welcome, Chosen One! You've died in the most pathetic way possible and been summoned to the world of Magisa! Choose your starting location wisely…"
- "uh… two."
- "Excellent choice! Absolutely nobody picks that one!"

41.content:
Name: The Siege of Fort Bramblehold (event)
